name: Adventuring Time
description: Discover every biome
hint: Visit every biome in the Overworld. Travel in all directions, cross oceans, climb mountains, and explore caves to find all biome variants. Use the F3 screen to track which biome you are in.
requirements: Visit all Overworld biomes.
tutorial:
You must visit every required overworld biome. There are approximately 52 biomes to discover. This requires extensive exploration across all terrain types.

Strategies:
- **Use /locatebiome** in a creative copy of your world to find missing biomes.
- **Nether travel** to cover ground faster (875 Nether blocks = 7000 Overworld blocks).
- **Track progress** in the advancement tab — hover over the advancement to see your completion count.
- **Rare biomes** to watch for: mushroom fields (ocean-surrounded), eroded badlands, bamboo jungle, ice spikes, snowy slopes, frozen peaks, jagged peaks, stony peaks, cherry grove, deep dark.
- **Use elytra + fireworks** for fast aerial exploration.
- Some biomes only exist at specific heights/terrain — peaks biomes need mountains, deep dark is below Y=0.

name: Arbalistic
description: Kill five unique mobs with one crossbow shot
hint: Enchant a crossbow with Piercing IV and line up five different types of mobs. Shoot the arrow so it passes through all five. Trapping mobs in a narrow line helps.
requirements: Kill 5 different mob types with a single piercing crossbow shot.
tutorial:
This requires a Piercing IV crossbow and extremely precise setup.

1. **Gather 5 different mob types** and line them up in a narrow corridor (use boats, leads, or a channel).
2. **Weaken all mobs** to 1 HP (use potions of Harming, or hit them carefully). Each mob must die from the single arrow.
3. **Line them up perfectly** — all 5 must be in a straight line within the arrow's path.
4. **Fire one shot** through all 5.

Recommended mob types: Use small-hitbox mobs that are easy to capture. Chickens, rabbits, and baby mobs work well. Build a narrow 1-block-wide corridor and pack them in.

The difficulty is that the arrow must kill each mob it passes through, and Piercing IV only passes through 5 entities total. Make sure they're all at 1 HP.

name: Sneak 100
description: Sneak near a Sculk Sensor or Warden to prevent it from detecting you
hint: Find a sculk sensor in the deep dark biome or craft one. Sneak (hold shift) near it to avoid triggering vibrations. You can also sneak near a warden to avoid detection.
requirements: Sneak near a sculk sensor or warden to avoid detection by staying crouched.
tutorial: Find a sculk sensor (deep dark biome or ancient city) and crouch-walk (sneak) near it. Sneaking prevents vibrations that would trigger the sensor.

name: Blowback
description: Kill a Breeze with a deflected Breeze-shot Wind Charge
hint: When a breeze in a trial chamber shoots a wind charge at you, hit it back (like deflecting a ghast fireball) to kill the breeze with its own projectile.
requirements: Kill a breeze by deflecting its own wind charge back at it.
tutorial: Found in trial chambers, the breeze shoots wind charges that can be deflected back by hitting them. Time your melee attack when the wind charge is close and aim it back at the breeze. The breeze has 30 HP, so this will take multiple reflected charges. Similar to ghast fireball deflection but wind charges deal less damage.

name: Isn't It Scute?
description: Get Armadillo Scutes from an Armadillo using a Brush
hint: Find an armadillo in a savanna or badlands biome. Use a brush on it to collect armadillo scutes, which can be used to craft wolf armor.
requirements: Use a brush on an armadillo to obtain armadillo scutes.
tutorial: Find an armadillo in a savanna or badlands biome. Craft a brush from a feather, copper ingot, and stick. Walk up to the armadillo slowly — if it curls up, wait for it to unroll. Use the brush on it to collect armadillo scutes, which are used to craft wolf armor.

name: Bullseye
description: Hit the bullseye of a Target block from at least 30 meters away
hint: Craft a target block from 4 redstone dust and 1 hay bale. Place it and move at least 30 blocks away. Shoot a fully charged arrow at the center (bullseye) to get a redstone signal strength of 15.
requirements: Hit the exact center of a target block with a projectile from at least 30 blocks away.
tutorial: Craft a target block (4 redstone + 1 hay bale). Place it and walk 30+ blocks away. Shoot the exact center with an arrow. The target block emits a redstone signal proportional to accuracy — full signal (15) means bullseye. Fully charged bow + practice. A flat area with clear line of sight is ideal.

name: Careful Restoration
description: Make a Decorated Pot out of 4 Pottery Sherds
hint: Collect 4 pottery sherds by brushing suspicious blocks in various structures. Craft a decorated pot at a crafting table using all 4 sherds (no bricks).
requirements: Craft a decorated pot using 4 pottery sherds (no brick substitutes).
tutorial: Find 4 pottery sherds from suspicious sand/gravel in trail ruins, desert pyramids, desert wells, ocean ruins, or cold ocean ruins. Use a brush (feather + copper ingot + stick) on suspicious blocks to extract sherds. Craft 4 sherds together in a diamond pattern in a crafting table. All 4 must be sherds — no plain bricks.

name: Crafters Crafting Crafters
description: Be near a Crafter when it crafts a Crafter
hint: Obtain a crafter from a trial chamber. Set it up with the materials to craft another crafter (iron ingots, crafting table, redstone dust, dropper). Activate it with a redstone signal while standing nearby.
requirements: Be within range of a crafter block when it crafts another crafter.
tutorial: Find a crafter block in a trial chamber or craft one from 5 iron ingots, 1 crafting table, 2 redstone dust, and 1 dropper. Place the crafter and load it with the recipe for another crafter. Power it with a redstone signal (lever or button) while standing nearby. You must be within range when the crafter finishes crafting.

name: Caves & Cliffs
description: Free fall from the top of the world (build limit) to the bottom of the world and survive
hint: Build up to the build limit (Y=320), then jump down to the minimum height (Y=-64) and survive. Use water at the bottom, a hay bale, slime blocks, or a potion of slow falling to survive the landing.
requirements: Fall from Y=320 to Y=-64 and survive.
tutorial:
You need to fall from build height (Y=320) to near bedrock (Y=-62) and survive.

Methods to survive:
1. **Water bucket MLG** — Place water at the bottom just before landing. Requires precise timing.
2. **Slow Falling potion** — Drink just before impact (but you still need enough fall distance).
3. **Hay bales** — Reduce fall damage by 80%. Stack enough at the bottom.
4. **Twisting vines / scaffolding** — Place at the bottom to break your fall.

Setup: Build up to Y=320 (bring lots of blocks), dig a clear shaft down to Y=-62 or lower. The easiest method is to place water at the bottom and aim for it. Use Feather Falling IV boots + a Totem of Undying as backup.

name: Heart Transplanter
description: Place a Creaking Heart with the correct alignment between two Pale Oak Log blocks
hint: Find a creaking heart block in the pale garden biome. Place it between two pale oak log blocks with the correct axis alignment to activate it.
requirements: Place a creaking heart between two pale oak logs in the correct orientation.
tutorial: Locate a pale garden biome and find a creaking heart block inside pale oak trees. Mine it with Silk Touch. Place two pale oak logs vertically, then place the creaking heart between them so its axis aligns with the logs. When placed correctly at night, a creaking mob will spawn.

name: Hero of the Village
description: Successfully defend a village from a raid
hint: Kill a raid captain to get Bad Omen, then enter a village to trigger a raid. Defeat all waves of pillagers, vindicators, ravagers, evokers, and witches to become the Hero of the Village.
requirements: Successfully complete a village raid by killing all raiders in every wave.
tutorial:
1. **Get Bad Omen** by killing a pillager captain.
2. **Enter a village** to trigger the raid.
3. **Survive all waves** — Normal difficulty has 5 waves, Hard has 7. Waves include pillagers, vindicators, ravagers, evokers, and witches.

Preparation tips:
- **Wall off the village** or funnel mobs through chokepoints.
- **Ring a bell** to highlight raiders with Glowing.
- **Bring strong gear**: Full diamond/netherite, Sharpness V sword, bow, shield, golden apples.
- **Protect villagers** — if all villagers die, the raid fails.
- **Totems of Undying** (dropped by evokers in earlier waves) are your insurance.

Reward: Hero of the Village gives massive trade discounts from villagers and triggers gift-throwing from villagers.

name: Sticky Situation
description: Jump into a Honey Block to break your fall
hint: Craft honey blocks from 4 honey bottles. Place a honey block on a wall and slide down it by jumping against it while falling. The honey block slows your descent.
requirements: Slide down the side of a honey block to slow your fall.
tutorial: Place a honey block on a wall (or stack them) and walk off an edge while pressed against it. Crafted from 4 honey bottles. You can also find honey blocks near bee nests.

name: Monster Hunter
description: Kill any hostile monster
hint: Kill any hostile mob such as a zombie, skeleton, spider, or creeper. They spawn in dark areas or at night.
requirements: Kill any hostile mob.
tutorial: Kill any hostile mob — zombie, skeleton, spider, creeper, etc.

name: Monsters Hunted
description: Kill one of every hostile monster
hint: Kill one of every hostile mob in the game. This includes mobs across all dimensions: overworld hostiles, nether mobs like blazes, ghasts, and piglins, and end mobs like shulkers and the ender dragon.
requirements: Kill at least one of every type of hostile mob in the game.
tutorial:
You must kill at least one of every required hostile mob in the game. The full list includes:

Blaze, Cave Spider, Creeper, Drowned, Elder Guardian, Ender Dragon, Enderman, Endermite, Evoker, Ghast, Guardian, Hoglin, Husk, Magma Cube, Phantom, Piglin, Piglin Brute, Pillager, Ravager, Shulker, Silverfish, Skeleton, Slime, Spider, Stray, Vex, Vindicator, Witch, Wither, Wither Skeleton, Zoglin, Zombie, Zombie Villager, Breeze.

Notable hard-to-find mobs:
- **Elder Guardian**: Ocean monument (3 per monument, don't respawn)
- **Evoker/Vex**: Woodland mansion or raids
- **Hoglin**: Crimson forest (Nether)
- **Piglin Brute**: Bastion remnants only (don't respawn)
- **Endermite**: 5% chance when throwing an ender pearl
- **Zoglin**: Bring a hoglin through a portal to the Overworld
- **Wither**: Must be summoned
- **Breeze**: Trial chambers

Track your progress in the advancement tab. This requires visiting nearly every structure and dimension in the game.

name: It Spreads
description: Kill a mob near a Sculk Catalyst
hint: Find a sculk catalyst in the deep dark biome or mine one with silk touch. Kill any mob near it and watch the sculk spread from the mob's death location.
requirements: Kill a mob within 8 blocks of a sculk catalyst, causing sculk to spread.
tutorial: Find a sculk catalyst in the deep dark biome or an ancient city (or silk touch one and bring it). Kill any XP-dropping mob within 8 blocks of it. The catalyst will absorb the XP and spread sculk blocks.

name: Lighten Up
description: Scrape a Copper Bulb with an Axe to make it brighter
hint: Find a copper bulb in a trial chamber or craft one. If it is oxidized (darker), use an axe on it to scrape off the oxidation layer and make it brighter.
requirements: Use an axe on an oxidized copper bulb to scrape off oxidation.
tutorial: Find a copper bulb in a trial chamber or craft one from 3 copper blocks, 1 blaze rod, and 1 redstone dust. If it has oxidized to a darker state, use any axe on it to scrape off one level of oxidation, making it brighter.

name: Surge Protector
description: Protect a Villager from an undesired shock without starting a fire
hint: Place a lightning rod near a villager during a thunderstorm. When lightning strikes the rod instead of the villager, the advancement triggers. The lightning must strike within 4 blocks of a villager but be redirected to the rod.
requirements: Have a lightning rod divert a lightning strike that would have hit a villager within a certain range.
tutorial:
Place a lightning rod within 128 blocks of a villager. During a thunderstorm, the rod must redirect a natural lightning strike that would have hit the villager. This is largely luck-based — you need a thunderstorm and lightning to strike near a villager.

Setup: Place a villager in an exposed area and surround them with lightning rods on slightly higher ground. Wait for a thunderstorm. Using a Channeling trident does NOT count — it must be natural lightning redirected by the rod.

name: Minecraft: Trial(s) Edition
description: Step foot in a Trial Chamber
hint: Find a trial chamber underground. They generate deep underground (commonly around Y=-20 to -40). Use an ominous map from a cartographer or just explore caves to find one.
requirements: Enter a trial chamber structure.
tutorial: Trial chambers generate underground, typically between Y=-40 and Y=-20. Use the /locate command in creative to find them, or look for copper and tuff structures while deep mining. They contain trial spawners and the breeze mob.

name: Ol' Betsy
description: Shoot a Crossbow
hint: Craft a crossbow from sticks, iron ingot, string, and a tripwire hook. Load it by holding right-click, then release and click again to shoot.
requirements: Shoot a crossbow.
tutorial: Craft a crossbow (3 sticks + 2 string + 1 iron ingot + 1 tripwire hook) or find one from pillagers. Right-click to charge, right-click again to fire.

name: Over-Overkill
description: Deal 50 hearts of damage in a single hit using the Mace
hint: Obtain a mace from trial chamber vaults. Build up very high (100+ blocks), then fall and hit a mob with the mace just before landing. The mace deals increasing damage the farther you fall.
requirements: Deal 50 hearts (100 damage) in a single mace hit.
tutorial:
The mace gains bonus damage based on fall distance. You need an enormous fall to deal 100+ damage.

1. **Obtain a mace** — Crafted from a breeze rod (dropped by breeze in trial chambers) + heavy core (from ominous vault in trial chambers).
2. **Build up HIGH** — You need roughly 100+ blocks of fall height.
3. **Fall and smash** — The mace negates fall damage to the user when it hits, so jump from a tall pillar and strike a mob at the bottom.
4. **Density enchantment** significantly increases mace damage per block fallen — highly recommended.
5. **Wind Burst enchantment** lets you bounce back up after hitting, but isn't needed for this advancement.

Target a high-HP mob (iron golem, ravager) to ensure it registers the full damage in one hit.

name: Sound of Music
description: Make the Meadows come alive with the sound of music from a Jukebox
hint: Craft a jukebox from 8 planks and 1 diamond. Find a meadow biome (typically on mountain slopes), place the jukebox, and insert any music disc.
requirements: Play a music disc in a jukebox placed in a meadow biome.
tutorial: Find a meadow biome (gentle flower-filled hills usually near mountains) and place a jukebox (8 planks + 1 diamond). Insert any music disc. Music discs drop when a skeleton kills a creeper, or can be found in dungeon/ancient city chests.

name: The Power of Books
description: Read the power signal of a Chiseled Bookshelf using a Comparator
hint: Craft a chiseled bookshelf from planks and slabs. Place a book in it, then place a redstone comparator next to it and check the signal output.
requirements: Place a comparator next to a chiseled bookshelf and read its redstone signal.
tutorial: Craft a chiseled bookshelf (6 planks + 3 slabs). Place books in it, put a redstone comparator behind it. The comparator outputs a signal strength based on which slot was last interacted with (1–6).

name: Revaulting
description: Unlock an Ominous Vault with an Ominous Trial Key
hint: Drink an ominous bottle or get Bad Omen and enter a trial chamber. Defeat the ominous trial spawner mobs to earn an ominous trial key. Use it on an ominous vault for rare loot.
requirements: Use an ominous trial key to open an ominous vault block.
tutorial: Get the Bad Omen effect by drinking an ominous bottle (found in pillager outpost chests or dropped by raid captains). Enter a trial chamber — the Bad Omen converts to Trial Omen. Defeat the ominous trial spawner mobs (tougher variants) to earn an ominous trial key. Use it on an ominous vault (the darker, decorated vault blocks) for rare exclusive loot like heavy core, wind burst, and more.

name: Adventure
description: Adventure, exploration and combat
hint: This advancement is granted automatically when you kill any mob or any mob kills you.
requirements: Kill any entity or be killed by any entity.
tutorial: Kill any mob or get killed. This happens within minutes of starting.

name: Respecting the Remnants
description: Brush a Suspicious block to obtain a Pottery Sherd
hint: Craft a brush from a feather, copper ingot, and stick. Find suspicious sand or suspicious gravel in structures like desert temples, desert wells, ocean ruins, or trail ruins. Use the brush on the suspicious block to reveal a pottery sherd.
requirements: Brush a suspicious sand or gravel block to obtain a pottery sherd.
tutorial: Craft a brush (feather + copper ingot + stick). Find suspicious sand or suspicious gravel in trail ruins, desert temples, desert wells, or ocean ruins. Right-click the suspicious block with the brush — hold it steady until the item is extracted. Some yield pottery sherds.

name: Take Aim
description: Shoot something with an Arrow
hint: Craft a bow from sticks and string, and craft arrows from flint, sticks, and feathers. Hold right-click to draw the bow, release to shoot and hit any entity.
requirements: Hit an entity with an arrow shot from a bow or crossbow.
tutorial: Craft a bow (3 sticks + 3 string) and arrows (1 flint + 1 stick + 1 feather), then shoot any mob.

name: Sweet Dreams
description: Sleep in a Bed to change your respawn point
hint: Craft a bed from 3 wool and 3 planks. Place it and right-click on it at night or during a thunderstorm to sleep and set your respawn point.
requirements: Sleep in a bed successfully.
tutorial: Craft a bed (3 wool + 3 planks) and sleep in it at night.

name: Sniper Duel
description: Kill a Skeleton from at least 50 meters away
hint: Find a skeleton and move at least 50 blocks away from it. Use a fully charged bow shot to kill it from that distance. A flat area with good visibility works best.
requirements: Kill a skeleton with a projectile from at least 50 blocks away.
tutorial: Find a skeleton in a flat area. Walk 50+ blocks away (measure with F3 coordinates). Shoot it with a bow — a fully charged Power V bow can one-shot a skeleton. Account for arrow drop at distance. Using a spyglass to verify distance beforehand helps.

name: Mob Kabob
description: Hit five mobs in the same Charge attack using the Spear
hint: Obtain a spear and use its charge attack to pierce through five mobs in a single charge. Line up groups of mobs in a tight corridor or pen for the best results.
requirements: Hit 5 mobs with a single spear charge attack.
tutorial: Craft a spear (a new 1.22 weapon) and use its charge attack. Line up at least five mobs in a tight space — a narrow corridor or animal pen works best. Initiate the charge attack to pierce through all five in a single charge.

name: Is It a Plane?
description: Look at the Ender Dragon through a Spyglass
hint: Bring a spyglass to the End dimension and use it to look at the Ender Dragon.
requirements: Look at the ender dragon through a spyglass.
tutorial: Bring a spyglass to the End and look at the dragon through it.

name: Is It a Balloon?
description: Look at a Ghast through a Spyglass
hint: Bring a spyglass to the Nether and use it to look at a ghast floating over lava lakes.
requirements: Look at a ghast through a spyglass.
tutorial: Use a spyglass to look at a ghast in the Nether.

name: Is It a Bird?
description: Look at a Parrot through a Spyglass
hint: Craft a spyglass from 2 copper ingots and 1 amethyst shard. Find a parrot in a jungle biome, then look at it through the spyglass by right-clicking.
requirements: Look at a parrot through a spyglass.
tutorial: Craft a spyglass (1 amethyst shard + 2 copper ingots). Find a parrot in a jungle biome and look at it through the spyglass.

name: Hired Help
description: Summon an Iron Golem to help defend a village
hint: Build an iron golem by placing 4 iron blocks in a T-shape and putting a carved pumpkin on top. Do this near a village to help defend it.
requirements: Summon an iron golem by building it from iron blocks and a carved pumpkin.
tutorial: Gather 36 iron ingots and craft 4 iron blocks. Find or carve a pumpkin (use shears on a pumpkin to get a carved pumpkin). Build a T-shape with the iron blocks: place one block on the ground, one on top, and one on each side of the top block. Place the carved pumpkin on top last. The iron golem will spawn immediately.

name: A Throwaway Joke
description: Throw a Trident at something. Note: Throwing away your only weapon is not a good idea.
hint: Obtain a trident as a rare drop from drowned mobs. Hold right-click to charge it, then release to throw it at any entity.
requirements: Throw a trident and hit an entity with it.
tutorial: Tridents are only obtained from drowned that are visibly holding them (about 6.25% of drowned hold them, 8.5% drop chance with Looting III). Once you have one, hold right-click to charge and release to throw it at a mob. Don't throw it into the void — use Loyalty enchantment to make it return.

name: Postmortal
description: Use a Totem of Undying to cheat death
hint: Obtain a totem of undying from an evoker (found in woodland mansions or during raids). Hold the totem in your main hand or off-hand and take lethal damage -- the totem will save you.
requirements: Be saved from death by a totem of undying.
tutorial: Totems of Undying drop from evokers (found in woodland mansions and raids). Hold the totem in either hand — when you take lethal damage, it activates automatically, restoring health and giving Absorption, Regeneration, and Fire Resistance. You must actually take lethal damage with it in hand.

name: What a Deal!
description: Successfully trade with a Villager
hint: Find a village and right-click on a villager with a profession (not a nitwit). Complete any trade by giving them the requested items.
requirements: Successfully complete a trade with a villager.
tutorial: Find a village and trade with any villager. Farmer villagers often have cheap first trades (wheat, potatoes, carrots).

name: Star Trader
description: Trade with a Villager at the build height limit
hint: Build a platform at the build height limit (Y=320) and transport a villager up there using minecarts, boats, or water elevators. Then trade with the villager at that height.
requirements: Complete a trade with a villager while at the build height limit (Y=320).
tutorial: Transport a villager to Y=319+ (build limit) and trade with them. Use a boat on scaffolding, a nether portal at high Y, or a long bubble column. The simplest method: place a villager in a boat, push the boat up a tall scaffolding tower to build height, and trade.

name: Smithing with Style
description: Apply these smithing templates at least once: Spire, Snout, Rib, Ward, Silence, Vex, Tide, Wayfinder
hint: Collect all 8 exclusive armor trim templates from their specific structures: Spire (end city), Snout (bastion remnant), Rib (nether fortress), Ward (ancient city), Silence (ancient city), Vex (woodland mansion), Tide (ocean monument), Wayfinder (trail ruins). Apply each one at a smithing table.
requirements: Apply all 8 exclusive armor trim smithing templates at least once each.
tutorial:
You need to find and use all 8 exclusive smithing templates, each from a different structure:

- **Spire**: End city
- **Snout**: Bastion remnant
- **Rib**: Nether fortress
- **Ward**: Ancient city
- **Silence**: Ancient city (very rare)
- **Vex**: Woodland mansion
- **Tide**: Elder guardian drop (ocean monument)
- **Wayfinder**: Trail ruins

The Silence trim is the rarest — it only appears in ancient city chests with a ~1.2% chance. Each template can be duplicated at a crafting table with 7 diamonds + the template + a specific material.

You need to visit 6 different structure types across 3 dimensions. Plan a systematic approach: Overworld structures first (trail ruins, woodland mansion, ocean monument), then Nether (fortress, bastion), then End (end city).

name: Crafting a New Look
description: Craft trimmed armor at a Smithing Table
hint: Find any armor trim smithing template in structures like trails ruins, desert temples, etc. Use a smithing table with the template, an armor piece, and a trim material (like copper, iron, gold, etc.).
requirements: Apply an armor trim to any armor piece using a smithing table.
tutorial: Find a smithing template (various structures have unique trim patterns) and use it at a smithing table with armor + a trim material (any ingot, crystal, etc.). The template, armor piece, and trim material go in specific slots. Templates are found in structures like trail ruins, desert pyramids, jungle temples, ocean monuments, and more.

name: Two Birds, One Arrow
description: Kill two Phantoms with a piercing Arrow
hint: Enchant a crossbow with Piercing, then stay awake for 3+ nights to spawn phantoms. Wait until two phantoms line up and shoot through both with one arrow.
requirements: Kill two phantoms with a single arrow that has the Piercing enchantment.
tutorial: Enchant a crossbow with Piercing (IV recommended for reliability). Stay awake for 3+ nights to spawn phantoms. Phantoms swoop in groups — wait for two to line up on a dive and shoot. This requires good timing and some luck. Piercing allows arrows to pass through entities, hitting multiple targets.

name: Under Lock and Key
description: Unlock a Vault with a Trial Key
hint: Defeat mobs from trial spawners in a trial chamber to earn a trial key. Use the trial key on a vault block to unlock it and receive loot.
requirements: Use a trial key to open a vault block in a trial chamber.
tutorial: Complete trial spawner challenges in a trial chamber to earn trial keys. Use a trial key on a vault block (the orange/copper blocks in trial chambers) to unlock it and receive loot. Each vault can only be opened once per player.

name: Country Lode, Take Me Home
description: Use a Compass on a Lodestone
hint: Craft or find a lodestone (crafted from a netherite ingot surrounded by chiseled stone bricks). Place it down, then use a compass on it. The compass will now point to the lodestone.
requirements: Use a compass on a lodestone block.
tutorial: Craft a lodestone (8 chiseled stone bricks + 1 netherite ingot) and right-click it with a compass. The compass will now point to the lodestone instead of world spawn. Lodestones work in all dimensions, making them invaluable for Nether navigation. The netherite ingot requirement makes this expensive.

name: Very Very Frightening
description: Strike a Villager with lightning
hint: Enchant a trident with Channeling. During a thunderstorm, throw the trident at a villager to summon lightning on them, converting them into a witch.
requirements: Hit a villager with a trident enchanted with Channeling during a thunderstorm, causing a lightning strike.
tutorial: Enchant a trident with Channeling. During a thunderstorm, throw the trident at a villager — the lightning will strike them, converting them into a witch. Note: this kills the villager's trading career, so use a villager you don't need.

name: Voluntary Exile
description: Kill a raid captain. Maybe consider staying away from villages for the time being...
hint: Find and kill a pillager captain (the one carrying the ominous banner) at a pillager outpost or during a patrol. This will give you the Bad Omen effect.
requirements: Kill a raid captain (pillager or vindicator carrying an ominous banner).
tutorial: Find a pillager outpost or patrol. The captain is the pillager carrying an ominous banner on its head. Kill it and you get the Bad Omen effect. Entering a village with Bad Omen triggers a raid.

name: Light as a Rabbit
description: Walk on Powder Snow... without sinking in it
hint: Craft leather boots and equip them. Find powder snow in snowy slopes or grove biomes, or collect it with a bucket. Walk on top of the powder snow while wearing leather boots to avoid sinking.
requirements: Walk on powder snow while wearing leather boots.
tutorial: Find powder snow (common in grove and snowy slopes biomes, or from cauldrons in snowy weather). Wear leather boots and walk on it — you'll walk on top instead of sinking.

name: Who Needs Rockets?
description: Use a Wind Charge to launch yourself upward 8 blocks
hint: Obtain wind charges from breezes in trial chambers. Look straight down, jump, and use the wind charge at your feet at the peak of your jump to launch yourself 8 blocks upward.
requirements: Use a wind charge to propel yourself upward at least 8 blocks.
tutorial: Obtain wind charges from breezes in trial chambers. Stand on flat ground, look straight down, jump, and immediately use a wind charge at your feet at the peak of your jump. The explosion launches you upward. You need to reach at least 8 blocks of height from where you used the charge. Timing the jump and charge together is key.

name: Who's the Pillager Now?
description: Give a Pillager a taste of their own medicine
hint: Kill a pillager using a crossbow. Find pillagers at outposts or patrols and shoot them with your crossbow.
requirements: Kill a pillager with a crossbow.
tutorial: Kill a pillager using a crossbow. Crossbows can be crafted or taken from dead pillagers.

name: You Need a Mint
description: Collect Dragon's Breath in a Glass Bottle
hint: While fighting the Ender Dragon, wait for it to use its breath attack or shoot acid fireballs that leave purple particles on the ground. Use a glass bottle on the lingering particle cloud to collect dragon's breath.
requirements: Collect dragon's breath by using a glass bottle on the dragon's breath particles.
tutorial: During the dragon fight (or a respawned dragon), the dragon breathes purple acid breath clouds on the fountain. Right-click the purple particles with an empty glass bottle to collect dragon's breath. Used to brew lingering potions. Bring several glass bottles — the clouds dissipate quickly.

name: The Next Generation
description: Hold the Dragon Egg
hint: After killing the dragon, the dragon egg appears on top of the exit portal. Don't click it directly (it teleports). Instead, push it with a piston, or place a torch below it and break the block it sits on.
requirements: Have the dragon egg in your inventory.
tutorial: After killing the dragon, a dragon egg appears on top of the exit portal. It teleports when clicked directly. To collect it: place a torch 2 blocks below it, then break the block the egg sits on. The egg falls onto the torch and drops as an item. Alternatively, push it with a piston.

name: Sky's the Limit
description: Find Elytra
hint: Find an end city with an end ship floating nearby. Inside the end ship, the elytra is displayed in an item frame. Grab it and equip it in your chestplate slot to glide through the air.
requirements: Have elytra in your inventory.
tutorial:
Elytra are found exclusively in end ships, which are attached to some (not all) end cities in the outer End islands.

1. **Find an end city with a ship** — Not all end cities have ships. The ship is a floating structure that looks like a boat, connected to the city by a bridge.
2. **Enter the ship** — The elytra is displayed in an item frame in the ship's hull. Watch out for shulkers.
3. **Take the elytra** — Right-click the item frame.

Elytra go in the chestplate slot. Activate by jumping while falling and pressing space. Use firework rockets (crafted with paper + gunpowder) to boost while flying. Mending and Unbreaking III enchantments are essential for long-term use.

name: Remote Getaway
description: Escape the island
hint: After defeating the Ender Dragon, an end gateway portal appears at the edge of the main island. Throw an ender pearl into it to teleport to the outer End islands.
requirements: Enter an end gateway portal.
tutorial: After killing the dragon, an end gateway portal spawns at the edge of the main island (small bedrock frame with a starry block inside). Throw an ender pearl into the starry block to teleport to the outer End islands. These islands contain end cities, chorus plants, and shulkers.

name: The City at the End of the Game
description: Go on in, what could happen?
hint: After reaching the outer End islands via an end gateway, explore to find an end city. These tall purpur structures contain valuable loot and may have an end ship with elytra.
requirements: Enter an end city structure.
tutorial: After using an end gateway, explore the outer End islands. End cities are tall purpur block structures that generate on the islands. They can be spaced far apart — bridge between islands using blocks or ender pearls. End cities contain valuable loot chests with enchanted gear, and some have end ships attached (which contain elytra).

name: Free the End
description: Good luck
hint: Defeat the Ender Dragon by destroying the end crystals on top of the obsidian towers first (use a bow or snowballs), then attack the dragon when it perches on the fountain. Bring a bow, plenty of arrows, and good armor.
requirements: Kill the ender dragon.
tutorial:
The ender dragon fight has two phases:

**Phase 1 — Destroy the end crystals:**
- 10 obsidian pillars each have an end crystal on top that heals the dragon.
- Shoot them with a bow (Infinity recommended) or climb the pillars.
- 2 crystals are in iron cages — you must pillar up and break the cage before destroying the crystal.
- Carry a water bucket for fall damage and to deal with endermen.

**Phase 2 — Kill the dragon:**
- The dragon perches on the central fountain periodically. This is when you deal the most damage — hit its head with a sword (deals bonus damage).
- Between perches, shoot it with arrows as it flies.
- Beds explode in the End and deal massive damage. Place a bed on the fountain's edge and click it when the dragon perches — stand behind an obsidian block for cover. This is the speedrunner strategy.
- The dragon has 200 HP.

**Gear recommendations:** Diamond/netherite armor (Prot IV), bow (Power V, Infinity), sword (Sharpness V), slow falling potions (critical for safety), ender pearls, water bucket, pumpkin head (prevents enderman aggro), golden apples, building blocks.

name: Great View From Up Here
description: Levitate up 50 blocks from the attacks of a Shulker
hint: Let shulker projectiles hit you in an end city to gain the Levitation effect. You need to levitate 50 blocks upward total. Stand at the bottom of a tall end city tower and let multiple shulkers hit you with their projectiles to float up.
requirements: Travel 50 blocks upward while under the Levitation effect from a shulker.
tutorial:
Shulker projectiles inflict Levitation, causing you to float up. To get 50 blocks of vertical distance:

1. Find a shulker in an end city with enough vertical clearance above it (at least 50 blocks of open air).
2. **Remove your armor** or wear minimal armor so the effect duration is longer (higher damage = longer effect).
3. **Let the shulker hit you** from a low point. Don't eat golden apples or drink milk.
4. You'll float up. Multiple hits stack the duration — let shulkers hit you repeatedly for more levitation time.
5. **Bring a water bucket or ender pearls** to survive the fall afterward, or use Slow Falling potions.

Alternative: Find a naturally tall end city tower and position yourself at the bottom with a shulker above. Some end cities have enough vertical space built in.

name: The End... Again...
description: Respawn the Ender Dragon
hint: Place 4 end crystals on the edges of the exit portal in the End (one on each side). This will respawn the Ender Dragon and regenerate the obsidian towers.
requirements: Respawn the ender dragon by placing 4 end crystals on the exit portal.
tutorial: Craft 4 end crystals (7 glass + 1 eye of ender + 1 ghast tear each). Place one on each side of the exit portal (on the edge blocks facing N/S/E/W). The dragon respawns, the crystals regenerate, and a new end gateway spawns after the fight. Note: each respawned dragon does NOT drop another dragon egg or as much XP.

name: The End
description: Or the beginning?
hint: Enter the End dimension through an End portal found in a stronghold. This advancement triggers automatically when you arrive.
requirements: Enter the End dimension.
tutorial: See 'The End?' in the Minecraft category.

name: Birthday Song
description: Have an Allay drop a Cake at a Note Block
hint: Give a cake to an allay, then place a note block nearby and play it. The allay will dance and drop the cake near the note block.
requirements: Have an allay drop a cake at a note block when it is played.
tutorial: Give a cake to an allay (right-click). Place and play a note block nearby. Drop a cake on the ground. The allay will pick up the cake and deliver it to the note block instead of to you, because it's heard the note block. The note block must have been played (clicked) at least once while the allay is nearby.

name: You've Got a Friend in Me
description: Have an Allay deliver items to you
hint: Find an allay in a pillager outpost cage or woodland mansion. Give it an item by right-clicking, and it will search for matching items nearby and bring them to you.
requirements: Have an allay deliver an item to you after giving it an item to search for.
tutorial: Find an allay in a pillager outpost cage or woodland mansion. Give it an item (right-click with the item). The allay will find matching items on the ground and bring them to you (or to a note block if one is playing nearby). Drop some of the same item on the ground nearby and the allay will pick them up and deliver them.

name: The Cutest Predator
description: Catch an Axolotl in a Bucket
hint: Find axolotls in lush cave biomes in underground water. Use a water bucket on an axolotl to catch it.
requirements: Use a water bucket on an axolotl to catch it in a bucket.
tutorial: Find an axolotl in a lush cave biome (underground, near azalea trees). Use a water bucket on it. Axolotls spawn in water above clay blocks in lush caves.

name: A Balanced Diet
description: Eat everything that is edible, even if it's not good for you
hint: Eat every edible item in the game at least once, including poisonous items like spider eyes, pufferfish, and rotten flesh. Some items like chorus fruit and enchanted golden apples are easy to miss.
requirements: Eat every unique food item in the game at least once.
tutorial:
You must eat every edible item in the game. Full list includes:

Apple, baked potato, beetroot, beetroot soup, bread, cake (place and eat), carrot, chorus fruit, cooked chicken, cooked cod, cooked mutton, cooked porkchop, cooked rabbit, cooked salmon, cookie, dried kelp, enchanted golden apple, glow berries, golden apple, golden carrot, honey bottle, melon slice, mushroom stew, poisonous potato, potato, pufferfish, pumpkin pie, rabbit stew, raw beef, raw chicken, raw cod, raw mutton, raw porkchop, raw rabbit, raw salmon, rotten flesh, spider eye, steak, suspicious stew, sweet berries, tropical fish.

Hardest items:
- **Enchanted golden apple**: Cannot be crafted, only found in loot chests (dungeon, mineshaft, desert temple, woodland mansion, ancient city, ruined portal). Very rare.
- **Poisonous potato**: 2% chance drop from potato crops.
- **Pufferfish**: Fish with a bucket or fishing rod in warm/lukewarm oceans.
- **Chorus fruit**: Found in End outer islands.
- **Suspicious stew**: Craft with mushrooms + flower + bowl, or milk a mooshroom after feeding it a flower.

Track progress in the advancement tab.

name: Two by Two
description: Breed all the animals!
hint: Breed every breedable animal in the game at least once. This includes cows, sheep, pigs, chickens, wolves, cats, horses, donkeys, llamas, rabbits, turtles, pandas, foxes, bees, goats, axolotls, frogs, camels, sniffers, and armadillos, among others.
requirements: Breed every breedable animal species at least once.
tutorial:
You need to breed every breedable animal in the game. Required animals and their breeding food:

- **Armadillo**: Spider eye
- **Bee**: Any flower
- **Camel**: Cactus
- **Cat**: Raw cod or raw salmon
- **Chicken**: Seeds (any type)
- **Cow**: Wheat
- **Donkey**: Golden apple or golden carrot
- **Fox**: Sweet berries or glow berries
- **Frog**: Slimeball (breeds tadpoles from frogspawn)
- **Goat**: Wheat
- **Hoglin**: Crimson fungus
- **Horse**: Golden apple or golden carrot
- **Llama**: Hay bale
- **Mooshroom**: Wheat
- **Mule**: Breed horse + donkey (golden apple/carrot)
- **Ocelot**: Raw cod or raw salmon
- **Panda**: Bamboo (must have 8+ bamboo nearby)
- **Pig**: Carrots, potatoes, or beetroots
- **Rabbit**: Carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions
- **Sheep**: Wheat
- **Sniffer**: Torchflower seeds
- **Strider**: Warped fungus
- **Trader Llama**: Hay bale
- **Turtle**: Seagrass
- **Wolf**: Any raw meat

Hardest to obtain: Mooshrooms (mushroom island), pandas (bamboo jungle, need bamboo nearby), sniffers (hatch from sniffer eggs in suspicious sand from warm ocean ruins), and trader llamas (from wandering traders — kill the trader and the llamas become tameable). Mules require both a horse AND donkey.

name: The Parrots and the Bats
description: Breed two animals together
hint: Find two of the same animal (cows, pigs, sheep, chickens, etc.) and feed each one their preferred food (wheat for cows/sheep, carrots/potatoes/beetroot for pigs, seeds for chickens) to make them breed.
requirements: Breed two animals of the same species together.
tutorial: Feed two animals of the same species their preferred food (e.g., wheat for cows/sheep, carrots for pigs, seeds for chickens). Hearts appear and a baby spawns.

name: A Complete Catalogue
description: Tame all Cat variants!
hint: Tame one of every cat variant. Stray cats spawn in villages and witch huts. There are 11 cat variants. Use raw cod or raw salmon to tame them by slowly approaching and feeding.
requirements: Tame all 11 cat variants.
tutorial:
There are 11 cat variants. Cats spawn in villages (max 10 per village) and are tamed with raw cod or raw salmon. Each cat has a random variant on spawn.

To get all 11: visit multiple villages, tame every cat you find. If you need specific variants, kill surplus cats to allow new ones to spawn. The 11 variants are: tabby, tuxedo, red, siamese, british shorthair, calico, persian, ragdoll, white, jellie, and black.

**Black cats** spawn exclusively during full moons or in swamp huts (witch huts). If you're missing the black cat, wait for a full moon near a village or find a swamp hut.

name: Little Sniffs
description: Feed a Snifflet
hint: Place a sniffer egg on moss or any block and wait for it to hatch (about 20 minutes, faster on moss). Feed the baby sniffer (snifflet) torchflower seeds.
requirements: Feed a baby sniffer (snifflet) its preferred food.
tutorial: Hatch a sniffer egg to get a snifflet (baby sniffer). Feed it torchflower seeds. Torchflower seeds are obtained by letting an adult sniffer dig — it sniffs the ground and digs up seeds. So you need at least one adult sniffer first, then hatch another egg or breed two adults.

name: Fishy Business
description: Catch a fish
hint: Craft a fishing rod from sticks and string. Cast it into any body of water and wait for the bobber to dip, then right-click to reel in a fish.
requirements: Catch any fish using a fishing rod.
tutorial: Craft a fishing rod (3 sticks + 2 string). Cast into water and wait for the bobber to dip, then right-click to reel in.

name: With Our Powers Combined!
description: Have all Froglights in your inventory
hint: Get all 3 froglight types (pearlescent, verdant, ochre) by having each frog variant eat a small magma cube. Bring each frog variant to the Nether near magma cube spawns. Temperate frogs produce ochre, cold produce verdant, warm produce pearlescent.
requirements: Have all 3 froglight variants in your inventory at the same time.
tutorial:
Froglights come in 3 colors based on frog variant. A frog eating a small magma cube produces a froglight:

- **Pearlescent (purple)**: Warm frog (white — spawns from tadpoles in warm biomes)
- **Verdant (green)**: Cold frog (green — spawns from tadpoles in cold biomes)
- **Ochre (yellow)**: Temperate frog (orange — spawns from tadpoles in temperate biomes)

Process:
1. Breed frogs with slimeballs to get frogspawn.
2. Collect tadpoles in buckets.
3. Release tadpoles in a warm, cold, and temperate biome to get all 3 frog variants.
4. Transport each frog to the Nether (boat through a portal).
5. Lead each frog near a magma cube spawner or small magma cubes. The frog must eat a SMALL magma cube (the smallest size).
6. Each frog produces its froglight color.

Alternatively, find natural frogs near swamps and transport them.

name: The Healing Power of Friendship!
description: Team up with an Axolotl and win a fight
hint: Release an axolotl from a bucket near an aquatic hostile mob (guardian, drowned, or elder guardian). When the axolotl attacks and you also fight the mob and it dies, you get regeneration and the advancement.
requirements: Kill a mob that an axolotl is also attacking, or have an axolotl assist you in combat.
tutorial: Release an axolotl from a bucket near a hostile aquatic mob (guardian, drowned, elder guardian). When the axolotl kills the mob or assists you, you receive Regeneration. The axolotl must be fighting alongside you — find a guardian or drowned and fight it with an axolotl nearby.

name: When the Squad Hops into Town
description: Get each Frog variant on a Lead
hint: There are 3 frog variants: temperate (orange), warm (white), and cold (green). Breed frogs or transport tadpoles to different temperature biomes (warm, temperate, cold) and let them grow. Use a lead on each variant.
requirements: Leash all 3 frog variants (temperate, warm, cold) with a lead.
tutorial: Obtain leads (4 string + 1 slimeball) and leash one of each frog type. You need a warm (white), cold (green), and temperate (orange) frog. Breed frogs in swamps (slimeballs), collect tadpoles, release them in warm/cold/temperate biomes to get all 3 variants. Leash all 3.

name: Glow and Behold!
description: Make the text of any kind of sign glow
hint: Obtain a glow ink sac by killing a glow squid (found in dark underwater areas). Place a sign with text, then use the glow ink sac on the sign to make its text glow.
requirements: Use a glow ink sac on a sign to make its text glow.
tutorial: Craft a sign and place it with text. Apply a glow ink sac (dropped by glow squids, found underground in water) to make the text glow. Right-click the sign with the glow ink sac.

name: Serious Dedication
description: Use a Netherite Ingot to upgrade a Hoe, and then reevaluate your life choices
hint: Craft a diamond hoe, then use a smithing table with a netherite upgrade template, the diamond hoe, and a netherite ingot to upgrade it to a netherite hoe.
requirements: Upgrade a diamond hoe to a netherite hoe using a smithing table.
tutorial: Use a smithing table with a netherite upgrade smithing template + diamond hoe + netherite ingot. The netherite ingot alone requires 4 ancient debris + 4 gold ingots. The most expensive hoe in the game.

name: Smells Interesting
description: Obtain a Sniffer Egg
hint: Find sniffer eggs by brushing suspicious sand in warm ocean ruins. The eggs are a rare find from archeology, so bring several brushes.
requirements: Have a sniffer egg in your inventory.
tutorial: Sniffer eggs are found by brushing suspicious sand in warm ocean ruins. Use a brush on suspicious sand blocks until you get a sniffer egg. Place the egg on moss for faster hatching (about 10 minutes on moss vs 20 on other blocks).

name: Stay Hydrated!
description: Place a Dried Ghast block into water
hint: Obtain a dried ghast block by finding one in a soul sand valley near nether fossils, bartering with piglins, or crafting from 8 ghast tears and 1 soul sand. Place the dried ghast block so it becomes submerged in water to rehydrate it into a happy ghast.
requirements: Place a dried ghast block into water.
tutorial: Obtain a dried ghast block — find them naturally in soul sand valleys near nether fossils, get one from piglin bartering, or craft from 9 ghast tears surrounding 1 soul sand. Bring it to the Overworld and place it so it touches water (a water source block or flowing water). It will rehydrate and become a happy ghast.

name: Planting the Past
description: Plant any Sniffer seed
hint: Let an adult sniffer sniff the ground -- it will eventually dig up ancient seeds (torchflower seeds or pitcher pods). Plant these seeds on farmland.
requirements: Plant a torchflower seed or pitcher pod on farmland.
tutorial: Let an adult sniffer roam on dirt/grass. It will periodically dig up ancient seeds (torchflower seeds or pitcher pods). Plant them on farmland. Sniffers dig roughly every 8 minutes.

name: A Seedy Place
description: Plant a seed and watch it grow
hint: Break tall grass to get wheat seeds, or harvest other crops. Use a hoe on dirt or grass to create farmland, then plant the seeds on the farmland near water.
requirements: Plant any type of seed on farmland.
tutorial: Use a hoe on dirt near water, then plant a seed (wheat seeds from breaking grass, or other crop seeds).

name: Shear Brilliance
description: Remove Wolf Armor from a Wolf using Shears
hint: Craft wolf armor from armadillo scutes and equip it on a tamed wolf. Then use shears on the wolf to remove the armor.
requirements: Use shears on a tamed wolf wearing wolf armor to remove the armor.
tutorial: Tame a wolf with bones, then craft wolf armor from 6 armadillo scutes. Equip the armor on your tamed wolf by using it on the wolf. To remove it, use shears on the armored wolf. The armor will drop as an item you can pick up.

name: Good as New
description: Fully repair damaged Wolf Armor using Armadillo Scutes
hint: Let your tamed wolf with wolf armor take some damage. Then use armadillo scutes on the wolf to repair its armor back to full durability.
requirements: Fully repair damaged wolf armor by using armadillo scutes on a wolf wearing the armor.
tutorial: Get armadillo scutes by brushing armadillos in savanna or badlands biomes. Equip wolf armor on a tamed wolf, then let the wolf take some damage (from mobs, fall damage, etc.) so the armor loses durability. Use armadillo scutes directly on the wolf to repair its armor. Keep using scutes until the armor is fully repaired.

name: Whatever Floats Your Goat!
description: Get in a Boat and float with a Goat
hint: Find a goat in a mountain biome. Place a boat near it and push or lure the goat into the boat, then get in the boat with it.
requirements: Ride in a boat with a goat as a passenger.
tutorial: Find a goat in a mountain biome. Place a boat near it and push or lead the goat into the boat, then get in yourself. Or place the boat in water and lure the goat in with wheat.

name: Husbandry
description: The world is full of friends and food
hint: This advancement triggers when you eat any food item or plant a seed for the first time.
requirements: Consume any food item or plant a seed.
tutorial: Eat any food item.

name: Bee Our Guest
description: Use a Campfire to collect Honey from a Beehive using a Glass Bottle without aggravating the Bees
hint: Place a campfire directly under or within 5 blocks below a bee nest or beehive. When the hive is full (honey_level 5), use a glass bottle on it to collect honey without angering the bees.
requirements: Collect honey with a glass bottle from a beehive or bee nest that has a campfire underneath.
tutorial: Find a bee nest (plains, flower forests, etc.) or craft a beehive. Wait until honey_level reaches 5 (dripping particles). Place a campfire under or within 5 blocks below the hive. Use a glass bottle on the hive to collect honey safely.

name: Total Beelocation
description: Move a Bee Nest or Beehive, with 3 Bees inside, using Silk Touch
hint: Enchant a tool (preferably an axe) with Silk Touch. Wait until nighttime or use smoke from a campfire so all 3 bees return to their nest, then mine the bee nest or beehive with the Silk Touch tool.
requirements: Mine a bee nest or beehive containing 3 bees using a Silk Touch enchanted tool.
tutorial: Find a bee nest with 3 bees living in it. Wait until nighttime or rain when all bees are inside. Mine the nest with a Silk Touch pickaxe. The bees stay inside the block. Smoke from a campfire makes bees enter the nest faster.

name: Tactical Fishing
description: Catch a Fish... without a Fishing Rod!
hint: Use a water bucket on a fish mob (cod, salmon, tropical fish, or pufferfish) swimming in the water to catch it in a bucket.
requirements: Catch a fish using a water bucket instead of a fishing rod.
tutorial: Use a water bucket on any fish mob (cod, salmon, tropical fish, pufferfish) swimming in the water. This picks them up as a bucket of fish.

name: Bukkit Bukkit
description: Catch a Tadpole in a Bucket
hint: Find frogs in a swamp biome and breed them with slime balls. They lay frogspawn that hatches into tadpoles. Use a water bucket on a tadpole to catch it.
requirements: Use a water bucket on a tadpole to catch it.
tutorial: Find frogs in a swamp or mangrove swamp biome. Feed two frogs slimeballs to make them breed — one will lay frogspawn on nearby water. Wait for the frogspawn to hatch into tadpoles, then use a water bucket on a tadpole to scoop it up.

name: Best Friends Forever
description: Tame an animal
hint: Tame a wolf with bones, a cat with raw cod or salmon, a horse by repeatedly mounting it, or a parrot with seeds. Hearts will appear when successfully tamed.
requirements: Successfully tame any tameable animal.
tutorial: Tame a wolf (bones), cat (raw cod or salmon), or horse (repeatedly mount until hearts appear). Wolves are the easiest — just give a wolf bones until hearts appear.

name: Wax Off
description: Scrape Wax off of a Copper block!
hint: Use an axe on any waxed copper block to scrape the wax off, allowing it to oxidize again over time.
requirements: Use an axe on a waxed copper block to remove the wax.
tutorial: Right-click a waxed copper block with any axe to remove the wax.

name: Wax On
description: Apply Honeycomb to a Copper block!
hint: Use shears on a beehive at honey_level 5 (with a campfire underneath to avoid bee aggro) to get honeycombs. Then use the honeycomb on any copper block to wax it and prevent oxidation.
requirements: Use a honeycomb on any copper block to wax it.
tutorial: Right-click any copper block with honeycomb. This prevents further oxidation. Honeycomb is obtained by using shears on a full beehive/nest (with campfire underneath).

name: The Whole Pack
description: Tame one of each Wolf variant
hint: Tame one wolf of each variant. Different wolf variants spawn in different biomes (forest, taiga, snowy taiga, grove, old growth spruce/pine taiga, etc.). Use bones to tame them.
requirements: Tame one of every wolf variant.
tutorial:
Wolf variants were added in 1.20.5 and are biome-dependent. There are 9 wolf variants:

- **Pale (classic)**: Taiga
- **Woods**: Forest
- **Ashen**: Snowy taiga
- **Black**: Old growth pine taiga
- **Chestnut**: Old growth spruce taiga
- **Rusty**: Sparse jungle / jungle
- **Spotted**: Savanna plateau
- **Striped**: Wooded badlands
- **Snowy**: Grove

All wolves are tamed with bones. You'll need to visit all these biomes and find wolves in each. Some variants are rarer due to lower spawn rates in their biome. Bring plenty of bones (skeleton farm recommended).

name: How Did We Get Here?
description: Have every effect applied at the same time
hint: Have every status effect in the game simultaneously. This includes all potion effects plus: absorption, bad omen, blindness, conduit power, darkness, dolphin's grace, glowing, haste, hero of the village, hunger, levitation, luck, mining fatigue, nausea, resistance, saturation, wither. This is extremely difficult and requires elaborate setup involving multiple mobs and sources.
requirements: Have every possible status effect applied at the same time (all 27+ effects).
tutorial:
This is widely considered the hardest advancement in the game. You need EVERY status effect active at the same time, including mob-inflicted ones.

All required effects (1.21.4):
- All 13 potion effects from 'A Furious Cocktail'
- **Absorption**: Golden apple
- **Bad Omen**: Kill a pillager captain
- **Conduit Power**: Activated conduit (requires prismarine + heart of the sea, must be near water)
- **Darkness**: Warden's sonic shriek
- **Dolphin's Grace**: Swim near a dolphin
- **Glowing**: Spectral arrow
- **Hero of the Village**: Win a raid
- **Hunger**: Husk attack or rotten flesh (unreliable) / Pufferfish
- **Levitation**: Shulker projectile
- **Mining Fatigue**: Elder guardian
- **Wither**: Wither boss or wither rose

Strategic approach:
1. **Location**: Build the attempt site in an ocean near an ocean monument. You need the elder guardian for Mining Fatigue and water for Conduit Power and Dolphin's Grace.
2. **Pre-work**: Win a raid for Hero of the Village (lasts 40 min). Get Bad Omen first, trigger raid, win it. Or reverse — get Hero first, then get Bad Omen from a caged pillager captain.
3. **Set up a shulker** — Transport a shulker from the End via minecart/boat through portals. This is one of the hardest parts.
4. **Set up a warden trigger** — Place sculk shriekers nearby to summon a warden for Darkness.
5. **Timing is everything** — Many effects are very short-duration (Dolphin's Grace, Levitation, Darkness). You need to trigger them nearly simultaneously.
6. **Drink all potions**, eat a golden apple (Absorption), step into a wither rose (Wither effect), get hit by the shulker (Levitation), swim past a dolphin (Dolphin's Grace), enter conduit range (Conduit Power), get shot by a spectral arrow from a skeleton with spectral arrows or a friend, trigger the warden (Darkness), and have an elder guardian nearby (Mining Fatigue).

Most players build an elaborate contraption with all mob sources in one spot and practice the sequence many times. Expect hours of setup and multiple attempts.

name: A Furious Cocktail
description: Have every potion effect applied at the same time
hint: Brew all 13 potion effects and apply them simultaneously. Use extended versions for longer durations. Drink them all quickly or have a friend help splash you. Effects needed: speed, slowness, strength, jump boost, regeneration, fire resistance, water breathing, invisibility, night vision, slow falling, poison, weakness, and turtle master.
requirements: Have all 13 status effects from potions applied at the same time.
tutorial:
You need all of these effects active at once: Speed, Slowness, Strength, Jump Boost, Regeneration, Fire Resistance, Water Breathing, Invisibility, Night Vision, Poison, Weakness, Slow Falling, Resistance.

Key strategy:
1. **Brew extended versions** of every potion possible to maximize duration windows.
2. **Use a beacon** for Resistance (the only reliable source) — requires defeating the Wither first. Set a beacon with a full iron/diamond/netherite pyramid for Resistance.
3. **Prep all potions** in advance and organize them in hotbar + inventory for fast drinking.
4. **Drink in order** — start with the shortest-duration effects last. Drink the longest-duration ones first.
5. **Conflicting effects**: Slowness and Speed can coexist. Poison and Regeneration can coexist. The trick is having them all overlap.
6. **Arrow of Weakness or Witch**: Weakness can come from a splash/lingering potion, or you can let a witch hit you.

Required effects and sources:
- Speed: Potion of Swiftness
- Slowness: Potion of Slowness
- Strength: Potion of Strength
- Jump Boost: Potion of Leaping
- Regeneration: Potion of Regeneration
- Fire Resistance: Potion of Fire Resistance
- Water Breathing: Potion of Water Breathing
- Invisibility: Potion of Invisibility
- Night Vision: Potion of Night Vision
- Poison: Potion of Poison
- Weakness: Potion/Arrow of Weakness
- Slow Falling: Potion of Slow Falling
- Resistance: Beacon only

Note: In 1.21.4, Wind Charging and Oozing/Infested are NOT required.

name: Local Brewery
description: Brew a Potion
hint: Craft a brewing stand using a blaze rod and cobblestone. Place water bottles in it, add nether wart to make awkward potions, then add an ingredient to brew a potion.
requirements: Pick up a potion from a brewing stand.
tutorial: Craft a brewing stand (1 blaze rod + 3 cobblestone). Place it, fill glass bottles with water, put them in the bottom slots, add blaze powder as fuel in the fuel slot, and add a potion ingredient in the top slot. Easiest potion: Awkward Potion (water bottle + nether wart). Most useful first brew: Potion of Fire Resistance (awkward potion + magma cream).

name: Not Quite "Nine" Lives
description: Charge a Respawn Anchor to the maximum
hint: Craft a respawn anchor from 6 crying obsidian and 3 glowstone blocks. Then use 4 glowstone blocks on it to fully charge it (4 charges max).
requirements: Charge a respawn anchor to full (4 charges) by using glowstone on it.
tutorial: Craft a respawn anchor (6 crying obsidian + 3 glowstone). Place it in the Nether and right-click it with glowstone blocks 4 times to fully charge it. Right-click it once more (empty-handed) to set your spawn. Each death consumes one charge. Crying obsidian comes from piglin bartering or ruined portals.

name: Bring Home the Beacon
description: Construct and place a Beacon
hint: Kill the Wither to obtain a nether star. Craft a beacon from 3 obsidian, 5 glass, and 1 nether star. Place it on top of a pyramid made of iron, gold, diamond, emerald, or netherite blocks.
requirements: Place a beacon block on a valid pyramid and activate it.
tutorial:
1. **Kill the Wither** to get a nether star (see 'Withering Heights').
2. **Craft a beacon**: 5 glass + 3 obsidian + 1 nether star.
3. **Build a pyramid** of iron, gold, emerald, diamond, or netherite blocks. Minimum: 9 blocks (3x3) for a 1-level pyramid. Full 4-level pyramid: 164 blocks (9x9 base). Iron is the most practical material.
4. **Place the beacon** on top of the pyramid. It must have a clear line of sight to the sky.
5. **Activate it** by inserting an iron ingot, gold ingot, diamond, emerald, or netherite ingot and selecting an effect.

name: Beaconator
description: Bring a Beacon to full power
hint: Build a full 4-layer pyramid under your beacon using 164 mineral blocks (iron, gold, diamond, emerald, or netherite). Iron blocks are the easiest to obtain in quantity.
requirements: Activate a beacon on a full 4-level pyramid (levels of 9x9, 7x7, 5x5, 3x3).
tutorial: Build a full 4-level pyramid (9x9, 7x7, 5x5, 3x3 = 164 blocks) of mineral blocks and place a beacon on top. Iron blocks are cheapest — you need 1476 iron ingots. An iron farm makes this much more feasible. Once the pyramid is complete and the beacon is placed, insert a mineral and select a primary + secondary power.

name: Oh Shiny
description: Distract Piglins with gold
hint: Drop or throw a gold ingot near a piglin, or give one to a piglin by right-clicking. The piglin will pick it up and examine it, dropping a random item in return.
requirements: Give a gold ingot to a piglin to distract it via bartering.
tutorial: Drop or right-click a gold ingot on an adult piglin. They'll examine it and give you a random item in return. Piglins are found in crimson forests and bastion remnants. Wear gold armor to prevent aggro.

name: Hot Tourist Destinations
description: Explore all Nether biomes
hint: Visit all 5 Nether biomes: nether wastes, soul sand valley, crimson forest, warped forest, and basalt deltas. Ride a strider to safely cross lava lakes between biomes.
requirements: Visit all 5 Nether biomes.
tutorial:
You must visit all five Nether biomes:

1. **Nether Wastes** — The classic red biome. Most common, hard to miss.
2. **Soul Sand Valley** — Blue fog, soul sand/soil, lots of skeletons and ghasts. Fossils and basalt pillars.
3. **Crimson Forest** — Red trees (huge crimson fungi), hoglins, piglins. Has crimson nylium floor.
4. **Warped Forest** — Teal/cyan trees (huge warped fungi), endermen spawn frequently. Safest biome.
5. **Basalt Deltas** — Gray, volcanic biome with magma cubes. Difficult terrain with lots of lava and blackstone.

Just travel through the Nether in a straight line. The biomes transition like Overworld biomes. The advancement triggers when you've set foot in all five. Use F3 to check your current biome.

name: Subspace Bubble
description: Use the Nether to travel 7 km in the Overworld
hint: Build two nether portals at least 875 blocks apart in the Nether (which equals 7000 blocks in the Overworld). Travel between them using the Nether.
requirements: Use the Nether to travel at least 7000 blocks in Overworld distance (875 blocks in the Nether).
tutorial: The Nether has an 8:1 distance ratio with the Overworld. Build a nether portal at your base, enter the Nether, travel 875+ blocks in any horizontal direction, build another portal, and exit. You'll emerge 7000+ blocks from your original Overworld position. Building a nether highway with ice and a boat is the fastest method. Packed ice or blue ice paths let boats travel extremely fast in the Nether.

name: Those Were the Days
description: Enter a Bastion Remnant
hint: Explore the Nether to find a bastion remnant, a large blackstone structure inhabited by piglins. They spawn in all biomes except basalt deltas.
requirements: Enter a bastion remnant structure.
tutorial: Bastion remnants generate in all Nether biomes except basalt deltas. They're large blackstone structures populated by piglins and piglin brutes. Just walk inside one. They come in four variants: bridge, hoglin stable, housing units, and treasure room. Be cautious — piglin brutes are permanently hostile and hit hard. Wear at least one piece of gold armor to prevent regular piglins from attacking.

name: A Terrible Fortress
description: Break your way into a Nether Fortress
hint: Explore the Nether to find a nether fortress -- a large dark structure made of nether bricks. They tend to generate in lines along the Z-axis.
requirements: Enter a nether fortress structure.
tutorial: Nether fortresses are large dark brick structures that generate in all Nether biomes. They tend to generate in strips along the Z-axis. If you can't find one, travel along the X-axis (east/west). Fortresses contain blaze spawners and wither skeletons, which are essential for progression.

name: Spooky Scary Skeleton
description: Obtain a Wither Skeleton's skull
hint: Kill wither skeletons in a nether fortress until one drops a wither skeleton skull. The drop rate is 2.5%, but Looting III increases it to 5.5%.
requirements: Have a wither skeleton skull in your inventory.
tutorial:
Wither skeletons spawn in nether fortresses at light level 0–7. They have a 2.5% chance to drop their skull on death, increased by Looting III to 5.5%. Strategies to farm efficiently:

1. **Looting III** is essential — almost doubles drop rate.
2. **Build a platform** in the fortress to concentrate spawns. Wither skeletons need a 3-block-tall space and nether brick to spawn on.
3. **Light up surrounding areas** to reduce other mob spawns and funnel spawns to your kill zone.
4. **Smite V** one-shots wither skeletons.
5. You need 3 skulls total to summon the Wither.

Expect to kill roughly 55 wither skeletons per skull without Looting, or ~18 with Looting III.

name: War Pigs
description: Loot a Chest in a Bastion Remnant
hint: Find a chest inside a bastion remnant and open it. Wear gold armor to prevent piglins from attacking you while looting.
requirements: Open a chest in a bastion remnant structure.
tutorial: Find a bastion remnant and open any chest inside. Wear gold armor to avoid piglin aggro, but be aware that opening chests or breaking gold blocks will anger piglins regardless. Piglin brutes are always hostile and cannot be pacified. Recommended approach: wear gold, locate a chest, grab loot, and be ready to fight or flee. The treasure room variant has the best loot including a guaranteed netherite upgrade smithing template.

name: Cover Me in Debris
description: Get a full suit of Netherite armor
hint: Smelt ancient debris into netherite scraps, combine 4 scraps with 4 gold ingots to make a netherite ingot. Use a smithing table to upgrade each diamond armor piece to netherite. You need 4 netherite ingots total (16 ancient debris).
requirements: Have a full set of netherite armor equipped (helmet, chestplate, leggings, boots).
tutorial:
You need 16 netherite scraps (16 ancient debris) + 16 gold ingots = 4 netherite ingots, plus 4 netherite upgrade smithing templates.

1. **Mine 16+ ancient debris** at Y=15 in the Nether (see 'Hidden in the Depths').
2. **Smelt them** into netherite scraps (furnace or blast furnace).
3. **Craft 4 netherite ingots**: 4 netherite scraps + 4 gold ingots each.
4. **Obtain 4 netherite upgrade smithing templates** — Found in bastion remnant treasure rooms. You only need to find 1, then duplicate it: 1 template + 1 netherrack + 7 diamonds = 2 templates.
5. **Upgrade each piece** at a smithing table: template + diamond armor piece + netherite ingot.

Total resources: ~16 ancient debris, 16 gold ingots, 4 diamond armor pieces, 4 templates (or 1 + duplication materials), and a smithing table.

name: Hidden in the Depths
description: Obtain Ancient Debris
hint: Mine ancient debris found at Y-level 8-22 in the Nether using a diamond pickaxe or better. Bed or TNT mining at Y=15 is the most efficient method.
requirements: Have ancient debris in your inventory.
tutorial:
Ancient debris generates in the Nether, most commonly at Y=15. Mining strategies:

1. **TNT mining**: The most efficient method. At Y=15, place TNT every 4 blocks in a line and chain-detonate. Ancient debris is blast-resistant, so it survives while other blocks are cleared.
2. **Bed mining**: Beds explode in the Nether. Place a bed at Y=15, stand behind a block for cover, and right-click the bed. Cheaper than TNT but more dangerous. Always place a blast-resistant block between you and the bed.
3. **Branch mining**: Mine at Y=15 with Efficiency V diamond/netherite pickaxe. Slower but safest.

Ancient debris spawns 1–3 per chunk, always surrounded by other blocks (never exposed to air). You need 4 ancient debris to make 1 netherite ingot.

name: Into Fire
description: Relieve a Blaze of its rod
hint: Kill blazes found in nether fortresses, especially near blaze spawners. They drop blaze rods which are essential for brewing and crafting eyes of ender.
requirements: Have a blaze rod in your inventory.
tutorial: Kill a blaze in a nether fortress. Blazes spawn naturally on fortress walkways and from blaze spawners. They shoot triple fireballs, so use a shield or strafe. Snowballs deal 3 damage to blazes and are cheap crowd control. A fire resistance potion makes the fight trivial. Blazes drop 0–1 blaze rods (increased by Looting).

name: Who is Cutting Onions?
description: Obtain Crying Obsidian
hint: Obtain crying obsidian by bartering with piglins (give them gold ingots), finding it in ruined portals, or trading with piglins in bastion remnants.
requirements: Have crying obsidian in your inventory.
tutorial: Crying obsidian can be found in ruined portals (Overworld and Nether) or obtained by bartering gold ingots with piglins (about 8.7% chance per barter, gives 1–3). It's used to craft respawn anchors.

name: Return to Sender
description: Destroy a Ghast with a fireball
hint: When a ghast shoots a fireball at you, hit it back by attacking (left-click) the fireball to deflect it. Aim carefully to hit the ghast with its own fireball.
requirements: Kill a ghast by deflecting its fireball back at it.
tutorial: When a ghast fires at you, time a melee hit (left-click) or shoot a projectile at the fireball to reflect it back. Melee is easier — punch the fireball when it's close. The fireball must kill the ghast, so aim carefully. The ghast has 10 HP and reflected fireballs deal up to 6 damage, so it may take 2 hits. Standing further away gives you more reaction time. A reflected fireball's trajectory follows your crosshair, so aim directly at the ghast.

name: This Boat Has Legs
description: Ride a Strider with a Warped Fungus on a Stick
hint: Find a strider on a lava lake in the Nether. Put a saddle on it, mount it, then hold a warped fungus on a stick to steer it across the lava.
requirements: Ride a strider while holding a warped fungus on a stick.
tutorial: Craft a warped fungus on a stick (fishing rod + warped fungus). Find a strider on a lava lake, put a saddle on it, mount it, and hold the warped fungus on a stick to steer. Warped fungus grows in warped forests.

name: Feels Like Home
description: Take a Strider for a loooong ride on a lava lake in the Overworld
hint: Bring a strider to the Overworld through a nether portal. Find a large lava lake (or create one), saddle the strider, and ride it for 50 blocks on lava using a warped fungus on a stick.
requirements: Ride a strider for 50 blocks on lava in the Overworld.
tutorial:
You need to bring a strider through a nether portal to the Overworld, then ride it across 50 blocks of lava. Steps:

1. **Saddle a strider** in the Nether and ride it into your portal.
2. **Find or create a lava lake** in the Overworld. Natural lava lakes exist underground and in certain biomes. You could also bucket-place a long lava channel.
3. **Ride the strider** with a warped fungus on a stick for 50+ blocks across the lava.

The biggest challenge is getting the strider through the portal and to a suitable lava source. Striders take damage from water and rain, so choose a non-rainy biome or go underground.

name: Nether
description: Bring summer clothes
hint: Enter the Nether by stepping through a lit nether portal. This advancement is granted automatically upon arrival.
requirements: Enter the Nether dimension.
tutorial: Same as 'We Need to Go Deeper' — build and enter a nether portal.

name: Withering Heights
description: Summon the Wither
hint: Place 4 blocks of soul sand or soul soil in a T-shape, then place 3 wither skeleton skulls on top. Build this in an enclosed area for a safer fight.
requirements: Summon the wither boss by placing the correct block pattern.
tutorial:
Collect 3 wither skeleton skulls (see 'Spooky Scary Skeleton') and 4 blocks of soul sand or soul soil.

Build a T-shape with soul sand/soil: place 3 blocks in a row, then 1 block on top of the middle block. Place the 3 wither skeleton skulls on top of the 3 upper blocks (place the middle skull last to trigger the spawn).

**CRITICAL WARNINGS**:
- Summon it far from your base — the Wither destroys blocks with its explosions.
- The Wither has 300 HP and two phases. Below half health, it gains Wither Armor (immune to arrows) and you must melee.
- The bedrock ceiling of the Nether (Y=127) can be exploited: summon the Wither under bedrock to trap its head, then melee it safely.
- Alternatively, fight it in a long underground tunnel. Smite V + Strength II makes quick work of it.
- Bring milk buckets to clear the Wither effect, golden apples for Absorption, and the best armor you have.

name: Uneasy Alliance
description: Rescue a Ghast from the Nether, bring it safely home to the Overworld... and then kill it
hint: Build a very large nether portal (the maximum size is 23x23) so a ghast can fly through it. Lure or push a ghast through the portal into the Overworld, then kill it.
requirements: Kill a ghast while in the Overworld dimension.
tutorial:
This is one of the most tedious advancements. You must lure a ghast through a nether portal and kill it in the Overworld.

1. **Build a massive nether portal** — Ghasts need a large opening. The portal frame can be up to 23x23 (interior 21x21). A 5x5 interior is the practical minimum, but bigger is better. Build it in an open area of the Nether where ghasts spawn (soul sand valley or nether wastes).
2. **Lure the ghast** — Ghasts wander randomly, so you need patience. Fishing rods can pull them. Position yourself so the ghast is between you and the portal. Lead entities don't work on ghasts.
3. **Get it through** — The ghast must fly into the active portal. This can take many attempts. Some players build a large funnel/tunnel structure around the portal to guide the ghast.
4. **Kill it in the Overworld** — Once it's through, kill it quickly before it floats away or despawns. Use a bow.

Alternative: Build a portal in a soul sand valley at ghast-flight altitude, create a long enclosed tunnel leading to the portal, and hope one floats in. Many players report this taking 1–3 hours of attempts.

name: Zombie Doctor
description: Weaken and then cure a Zombie Villager
hint: Throw a splash potion of weakness at a zombie villager, then feed it a golden apple. Wait a few minutes for the curing process to finish.
requirements: Cure a zombie villager by applying Weakness and then using a golden apple on it.
tutorial:
This requires two items and some patience:

1. **Find a zombie villager** — They spawn naturally (5% of zombie spawns) or you can let a zombie kill a villager (50% conversion on Normal, 100% on Hard).
2. **Throw a Splash Potion of Weakness** at the zombie villager. Brewing: Water Bottle → add Fermented Spider Eye = Potion of Weakness → add Gunpowder = Splash Potion of Weakness. Alternative: A witch may throw weakness at you while near the zombie villager, or use a tipped arrow.
3. **Use a golden apple** on the weakened zombie villager (right-click with a regular golden apple, NOT enchanted). Craft: 8 gold ingots + 1 apple.
4. Wait 3–5 minutes. The zombie villager will shake and emit red particles. Keep it safe from sunlight and other mobs during conversion. Iron bars and a bed nearby speed up conversion.

The cured villager will have deeply discounted trades and can restore its previous profession if its job site block is nearby.

name: Not Today, Thank You
description: Deflect a projectile with a Shield
hint: Craft a shield from iron ingot and planks, then hold right-click to block while a skeleton or other mob shoots a projectile at you.
requirements: Block a projectile with a shield.
tutorial: Craft a shield (1 iron ingot + 6 planks). Hold right-click to raise it when a skeleton shoots at you or a pillager fires a crossbow.

name: Enchanter
description: Enchant an item at an Enchanting Table
hint: Craft an enchanting table from 4 obsidian, 2 diamonds, and 1 book. Place it down, surround it with bookshelves for better enchantments, then enchant any tool, weapon, or armor piece.
requirements: Enchant an item using an enchanting table.
tutorial: Craft an enchanting table (4 obsidian, 2 diamonds, 1 book). Place it with bookshelves arranged in the standard pattern (1 block gap, up to 15 bookshelves for max level 30 enchants). You need lapis lazuli and experience levels to enchant. For the cheapest option, enchant a book or wooden tool at level 1 to unlock the advancement.

name: The End?
description: Enter the End Portal
